question: I just used /jetson-customize-pcie to commit a kernel-DT overlay change under Source/bsp_sources/. How do I rebuild the affected artifacts and prepare them for /jetson-promote-image?
ground_truth: Invoke /jetson-build-source with no argument so the dirty-repo detector inspects bsp_sources/, auto-picks the 'dt' mode (only hardware/nvidia/* is dirty), rebuilds the composite .dtbo via the upstream nvidia-dtbs target, and writes .build-manifest.yaml under <source.root_path>/ for /jetson-promote-image to consume on the next promote.
expected_behavior: Detect dirty source repos using the per-repo HEAD watermark in .build-state.yaml
Auto-pick dt mode because only hardware/nvidia/* (or kernel-devicetree) is dirty
Drive the upstream nvidia-dtbs make target against bsp_sources/, never duplicating its logic in shell
Emit .build-manifest.yaml listing only artifacts traceable to a dirty source repo (trace-to-dirty policy)
Update the watermark in .build-state.yaml on success

question: I ran /jetson-build-source on a fresh Ubuntu host and it refused with 'host package missing: libssl-dev'. What do I need to install, and which other packages are hard-required versus warn-only?
ground_truth: Install libssl-dev with 'sudo apt install libssl-dev' and re-invoke /jetson-build-source. flex, bison, and libssl-dev are hard prerequisites that refuse up-front because the kernel make chain fails at defconfig or link without them. git, build-essential, bc, and zstd are soft prerequisites that only emit a warning — the build will surface a clearer error if any are actually needed and missing.
expected_behavior: Read skills/jetson-build-source/SKILL.md and locate the Prerequisites bullet
Identify that libssl-dev is in the hard-required set (refuses up-front), not the warn-only set
Instruct the user to run 'sudo apt install libssl-dev'
Note that flex and bison are also hard-required and worth installing in the same apt invocation if not already present
